text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Wang
Saffron Park Hospital

Saffron Park, London, E14 7JL
Dear Dr Wang
John Doe, DOB: 21-11-1982, Hospital No: P546484 NHS No. 5484656746
Flat 15 Roundwood Road, London, E14 7JL 

I reviewed the above patient in the Neurology Clinic today. He works on a busy metro station gate line where prolonged standing and intermittent crowd surges are common, which he reports can increase general fatigue though not clearly linked to event precipitation. He carries a laminated safety plan at work as advised by Occupational Health.

He is known to have focal epilepsy, with focal impaired-awareness seizures arising from the right temporal region on prior EEG, and occasional sensory auras described as a brief metallic taste and déjà vu. By his account, there have been no witnessed focal impaired-awareness events or convulsions since late February 2025, and he reports no auras since early April 2025—effectively no clinical seizures reported in the intervening months. Colleague observations and his self-kept diary are in agreement. There have been no injuries or post-event confusion episodes. 

Current antiepileptic regimen: Lamotrigine 200 mg b.d., Levetiracetam 1 g b.d., and Lacosamide 100 mg b.d. Adherence is good; he uses a phone reminder and takes doses during scheduled breaks. Side effects are limited to mild morning light-headedness lasting a few minutes and occasional tingling in the fingertips, not progressive. He denies mood change, diplopia, rash, or gastrointestinal upset. Routine bloods in June 2025 were within reference, including LFTs and U&Es. 

We discussed risk in the context of his work environment. He stands for long periods, may need to turn rapidly during crowd surges, and sometimes works near metal barriers and ticket gates. He notes improved sleep hygiene over the last three months and reduced caffeine intake to one coffee daily. Hydration at work remains adequate; he keeps a water bottle at the gate line. There have been no episodes of unresponsiveness or automatisms reported by supervisors since February. He retains awareness of triggers and has a written escalation plan should any aura recur. 

Neurological examination today is unremarkable with normal gait and coordination, and there are no focal deficits. Given that events have not occurred by patient and workplace report for several months, and he remains clinically stable, I have not changed his medication today. We will continue with the current regimen and safety measures. I have arranged for an annual surveillance MRI (last performed July 2023) for completeness and will repeat an EEG only if symptoms recur. He is aware to contact us promptly should auras reappear or if there is any breakthrough event, particularly in the context of fatigue, intercurrent illness, or missed doses. 

We agreed a follow-up in six months, sooner if there is any change. A brief workplace liaison letter can be provided on request, outlining that he currently reports no clinical seizures and is stable on treatment, with standard safety precautions in place. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: there have been no witnessed focal impaired-awareness events or convulsions since late February 2025, and he reports no auras since early April 2025—effectively no clinical seizures reported in the intervening months.

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter states: no witnessed focal impaired-awareness events or convulsions since late February 2025, and no auras since early April 2025—effectively no clinical seizures in the intervening months. Clinic date is 02 October 2025. Calculate seizure-free duration for clinical seizures: from late February 2025 to early October 2025 is approximately 7+ months (March through September fully, plus part of February and October). This meets the ≥6-month criterion for seizure free status. Therefore, classify as seizure free for multiple months (since duration is months-level and exceeds 6 months).
seizure_frequency_number: there have been no witnessed focal impaired-awareness events or convulsions since late February 2025, and he reports no auras since early April 2025—effectively no clinical seizures reported in the intervening months.
